title: Caucasus Campaign (Case Blue — Army Group A)
summary: The Caucasus campaign was the southern half of Germany's 1942 summer offensive, aimed at capturing the Soviet oilfields at Baku, Maikop, and Grozny. Army Group A advanced rapidly but was stopped short of its objectives by terrain, distance, and the diversion of forces to Stalingrad.
objectives: Germany consumed 80% of its domestic oil supply annually; capturing Caucasus oilfields would both deny them to the Soviet Union and alleviate Germany's own critical oil shortage.

axis.strength: Army Group A: 1st Panzer Army, 17th Army

outcome: Failure. Maikop oilfields captured but destroyed before seizure; Grozny and Baku never reached. German advance halted in the Caucasus mountains, denied by terrain, overextended supply lines, and the catastrophe at Stalingrad which forced withdrawal.

casualties.soviet: ~590,000 killed, wounded, missing during the campaign
